title: dactylorhiza fuchsii subsp. sudetica
intro: Dactylorhiza fuchsii subsp. sudetica is a subspecies of the orchid plant, specifically a terrestrial orchid native to Europe, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and tall, slender stems. This plant is also known for its medicinal properties, with traditional uses including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza fuchsii subsp. sudetica include relief from respiratory problems, improved digestion, and reduced stress levels, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the roots and flowers, then using them in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy for human consumption.
